chapter_summary: Chapter 2 tells us how to find wisdom. Solomon says we have to really look for it — like hunting for hidden treasure. When we search for wisdom with our whole heart and ask God for it, He gives it! God is the one who has all wisdom. He gives it to people who love and trust Him. Wisdom also keeps us safe from people who want us to do wrong things.
main_lesson: Ask God for wisdom with all your heart. He will give it to you like a treasure.
memory_verse: For the LORD giveth wisdom: out of his mouth cometh knowledge and understanding.
parent_guide: Proverbs 2 establishes the effort involved in gaining wisdom — it requires seeking, asking, and listening. Key themes: (1) Wisdom is a gift from God, not just something earned through intelligence (v.6). (2) The search must be wholehearted — 'if thou seekest her as silver' (v.4). (3) Wisdom protects us morally — it guards the heart from bad paths. Help your child see that prayer is the starting point: asking God for wisdom before a hard day, a tricky decision, or a difficult conversation.
application_for_children: This week, when you face a hard choice, stop and pray: 'God, give me wisdom.' Then ask a parent or trusted adult for help. Look for wise people around you — they are God's gift to us.

verses.kjv: My son, if thou wilt receive my words, and hide my commandments with thee;
verses.little_bible: My child, if you listen to these wise words and keep them in your heart,
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Listen carefully and keep these words in your heart.
verses.meaning: Wisdom starts with listening and holding God's words close, like something very precious. This is the first step — being willing to receive what God says.
verses.memory_phrase: I keep God's words in my heart.
verses.prayer: God, help me listen carefully and remember Your words.
verses.discussion_question: How do you remember something really important?
verses.family_discussion: What is one of God's words you want to keep in your heart?
verses.do_it_today: Write or draw one thing God says and put it somewhere you will see it.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child holding a glowing scroll close to their chest, smiling warmly, soft golden light around them

verses.kjv: So that thou incline thine ear unto wisdom, and apply thine heart to understanding;
verses.little_bible: Lean your ear toward wisdom, and open your heart to understanding.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Lean in and listen. Let your heart learn.
verses.meaning: We must actively lean toward wisdom — bending our whole self toward learning what is right and true.
verses.memory_phrase: I lean toward wisdom.
verses.prayer: God, help me lean toward wisdom and open my heart to learn from You.
verses.discussion_question: What do you do with your body when you really want to hear something?
verses.family_discussion: What does it look like to lean toward God — with our ears, hearts, and minds?
verses.do_it_today: Next time someone wise is speaking, lean in a little and really listen.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child leaning forward with one ear tilted toward a glowing book, eyes bright with curiosity

verses.kjv: Yea, if thou criest after knowledge, and liftest up thy voice for understanding;
verses.little_bible: Call out for wisdom! Ask God loud and clear to help you understand.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Ask God to help you know what is right.
verses.meaning: We should ask for wisdom eagerly and loudly, the way we ask for something we really need. God hears and loves that kind of earnest asking.
verses.memory_phrase: I ask God for wisdom!
verses.prayer: God, I am asking You right now — please give me wisdom and understanding.
verses.discussion_question: What do you do when you really, really want something?
verses.family_discussion: Why is wisdom something worth asking for loudly?
verses.do_it_today: Pray together out loud right now: 'God, give us wisdom today!'
verses.illustration_prompt: A child with arms raised and eyes looking up to a bright sky, calling out to God

verses.kjv: If thou seekest her as silver, and searchest for her as for hid treasures;
verses.little_bible: Search for wisdom the way you would hunt for buried treasure. Look everywhere for it!
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Look for wisdom like you look for hidden treasure.
verses.meaning: Wisdom is worth searching for like precious treasure. The more we look, the more we find. This kind of searching means never giving up.
verses.memory_phrase: Wisdom is my treasure.
verses.prayer: God, help me love wisdom more than toys or treats.
verses.discussion_question: If you found a real treasure map, how hard would you search for the treasure?
verses.family_discussion: What would it look like to search for wisdom this week?
verses.do_it_today: Go on a 'wisdom hunt' — find one wise thing in the Bible or from a wise person today.
verses.illustration_prompt: A joyful child digging up a glowing treasure chest full of golden scrolls and stars

verses.kjv: Then shalt thou understand the fear of the LORD, and find the knowledge of God.
verses.little_bible: Then you will understand how amazing and holy God is, and you will really know Him.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: You will know God better when you search for wisdom.
verses.meaning: When we seek wisdom wholeheartedly, the reward is knowing God — the greatest treasure of all. Fearing God means loving and respecting Him deeply.
verses.memory_phrase: Wisdom helps me know God.
verses.prayer: God, as I search for wisdom, let me know You more and more.
verses.discussion_question: What does it mean to 'know' someone — really, truly know them?
verses.family_discussion: How does seeking wisdom bring us closer to knowing God?
verses.do_it_today: Read one verse from the Bible today as a way of getting to know God better.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child holding an open Bible, with warm golden light shining from the pages up to their face

verses.kjv: For the LORD giveth wisdom: out of his mouth cometh knowledge and understanding.
verses.little_bible: God is the one who gives wisdom. All real knowledge and understanding comes from Him.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: God gives us wisdom. He knows everything.
verses.meaning: All true wisdom starts with God. He is the source — without Him, we cannot truly understand what is right.
verses.memory_phrase: Wisdom comes from God.
verses.prayer: God, You know everything. Please share Your wisdom with me.
verses.discussion_question: Where does wisdom come from?
verses.family_discussion: How does knowing that God gives wisdom change the way we pray?
verses.do_it_today: Before making a decision today, stop and say: 'God, what do You think?'
verses.illustration_prompt: Rays of warm golden light coming from heaven down to a child reading the Bible

verses.kjv: He layeth up sound wisdom for the righteous: he is a buckler to them that walk uprightly.
verses.little_bible: God saves up good wisdom for people who do right. He is like a shield that protects them.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: God protects those who do what is right.
verses.meaning: God has stored up wisdom and protection for people who love Him and live rightly. He is their shield against harm.
verses.memory_phrase: God is my shield.
verses.prayer: God, protect me today as I try to do what is right.
verses.discussion_question: What does a shield do? How is God like a shield for us?
verses.family_discussion: How has God protected your family when you have done what is right?
verses.do_it_today: Think of one right thing you can do today, and trust God to protect you as you do it.
verses.illustration_prompt: A small child with a glowing golden shield of light all around them, standing safely and confidently

verses.kjv: He keepeth the paths of judgment, and preserveth the way of his saints.
verses.little_bible: God watches over the paths of justice and keeps His people safe on the right way.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: God keeps His people safe on the right path.
verses.meaning: God is not distant — He actively guards the road His people walk on. He watches over those who belong to Him.
verses.memory_phrase: God watches over my path.
verses.prayer: God, thank You for watching over me every step of the way.
verses.discussion_question: How does it feel to know God is watching over your path?
verses.family_discussion: Can you think of a time when God kept your family on the right path?
verses.do_it_today: Take a walk and with each step say: 'God is watching over this step too.'
verses.illustration_prompt: A child walking along a winding path, with God's large, gentle hand hovering softly overhead

verses.kjv: Then shalt thou understand righteousness, and judgment, and equity; yea, every good path.
verses.little_bible: Then you will understand what is right and fair and good — every good path will make sense to you.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Wisdom helps you know what is right and fair.
verses.meaning: Wisdom gives us the ability to understand fairness, justice, and right living — it helps us navigate every situation with clarity.
verses.memory_phrase: Wisdom helps me know what is right.
verses.prayer: God, help me understand what is right and fair in every situation.
verses.discussion_question: What does fair mean? Can you give an example of something fair and something unfair?
verses.family_discussion: Why is it important to understand what is right and fair?
verses.do_it_today: Look for one chance to be fair to someone else today.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child holding balanced scales, smiling, in a sunny garden full of bright paths ahead

verses.kjv: When wisdom entereth into thine heart, and knowledge is pleasant unto thy soul;
verses.little_bible: When wisdom fills your heart, it feels wonderful — like something good and sweet inside you.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Wisdom in your heart feels good and sweet.
verses.meaning: Wisdom is not just information — it feels good in our hearts when we love what is right and true.
verses.memory_phrase: Wisdom feels good in my heart.
verses.prayer: God, fill my heart with Your wisdom so it feels right and sweet.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever done the right thing and felt really good inside?
verses.family_discussion: What does it feel like when you make a wise choice?
verses.do_it_today: Do one kind, wise thing today and notice how it makes you feel.
verses.illustration_prompt: A smiling child with a warm glow coming from their heart, surrounded by gentle golden light

verses.kjv: Discretion shall preserve thee, understanding shall keep thee:
verses.little_bible: Wisdom will keep you safe. Understanding will protect you like a shield.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Wisdom keeps you safe like a shield.
verses.meaning: When we have wisdom, it protects us from making choices that would hurt us or others.
verses.memory_phrase: Wisdom keeps me safe.
verses.prayer: God, let Your wisdom be my protection every day.
verses.discussion_question: How can knowing what is right keep you out of trouble?
verses.family_discussion: Tell about a time when making a wise choice kept someone safe.
verses.do_it_today: Think of one risky or unkind thing you will say no to this week because of wisdom.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child walking safely on a bright path, with a gentle golden shield of light around them

verses.kjv: To deliver thee from the way of the evil man, from the man that speaketh froward things;
verses.little_bible: Wisdom will rescue you from people who do wrong and say hurtful, twisted things.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Wisdom keeps you away from people who say wrong things.
verses.meaning: One of wisdom's great gifts is helping us recognise and avoid people whose words and actions lead us away from what is good.
verses.memory_phrase: Wisdom helps me avoid bad paths.
verses.prayer: God, help me recognise wrong and keep me away from it.
verses.discussion_question: What should you do if someone asks you to do something wrong?
verses.family_discussion: How does wisdom help us say no when someone asks us to do something bad?
verses.do_it_today: Practise saying 'No, that's not right' — so it feels natural when you need to say it for real.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child calmly turning away from a dark, tangled path and walking toward a bright open one

verses.kjv: Who leave the paths of uprightness, to walk in the ways of darkness:
verses.little_bible: Some people leave the right path and walk into the dark — they choose to do wrong on purpose.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Some people leave the right path and go into the dark.
verses.meaning: Choosing darkness is always a decision — people who do wrong have left the right path that was available to them.
verses.memory_phrase: I stay on the bright path.
verses.prayer: God, help me never leave the right path and choose darkness.
verses.discussion_question: What happens when you walk in the dark without a light?
verses.family_discussion: Why is it so important to stay on God's bright path?
verses.do_it_today: Read a Bible verse that lights up your path today — maybe Psalm 119:105.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two paths: one glowing and bright, one dark and tangled — a child firmly choosing the bright one

verses.kjv: Who rejoice to do evil, and delight in the frowardness of the wicked;
verses.little_bible: These people actually enjoy doing wrong things and love being wicked — that is how lost they are.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Some people enjoy doing wrong. That is very sad.
verses.meaning: When someone loves doing wrong instead of feeling sorry for it, they have gone far from God's wisdom. It is a warning for us to stay close to God.
verses.memory_phrase: I love what is good.
verses.prayer: God, help me always love what is good and right, and never enjoy doing wrong.
verses.discussion_question: How do you feel inside when you do something wrong — happy or uncomfortable?
verses.family_discussion: Why does loving good things protect us from going the wrong way?
verses.do_it_today: Name three good things you love — thank God for them.
verses.illustration_prompt: A split image: a sad dark scene of someone making wrong choices, and a bright joyful child choosing good

verses.kjv: Whose ways are crooked, and they froward in their paths:
verses.little_bible: Their ways are all twisted and bent — they are always going the wrong direction.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Their paths are twisted and wrong.
verses.meaning: A crooked path cannot take you to a good place. People whose ways are twisted need to turn back to God's straight path.
verses.memory_phrase: God's path is straight and good.
verses.prayer: God, keep my path straight and clear.
verses.discussion_question: What happens if you take a twisted, wrong path to school?
verses.family_discussion: How do we know if the path we are on is straight or crooked?
verses.do_it_today: Draw a straight bright path and a crooked dark one — think about which one you want to walk on.
verses.illustration_prompt: A bright straight path glowing ahead, with tangled crooked paths off to the sides

verses.kjv: To deliver thee from the strange woman, even from the stranger which flattereth with her words;
verses.little_bible: Wisdom will also protect you from those who say flattering, sweet things just to lead you astray.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Wisdom protects you from people who say nice things to trick you.
verses.meaning: Some people use kind-sounding words to lead others into danger. Wisdom helps us recognise flattery that is not honest and protect ourselves from it.
verses.memory_phrase: Wisdom protects me from tricky words.
verses.prayer: God, help me recognise when kind words are not true, and protect me from being tricked.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever heard someone say really sweet things but something felt a little wrong?
verses.family_discussion: How can we tell the difference between real kindness and flattery that is trying to trick us?
verses.do_it_today: Ask a parent: 'What should I do if someone says really nice things but wants me to do something wrong?'
verses.illustration_prompt: A child pausing and looking thoughtfully, with a gentle adult guiding them away from a smiling but shadowy figure

verses.kjv: Which forsaketh the guide of her youth, and forgetteth the covenant of her God.
verses.little_bible: This is about someone who left the good teaching they grew up with and forgot the promises they made to God.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Some people forget God's good ways that they learned when they were young.
verses.meaning: Forgetting God and the good teaching of our early years is dangerous. Holding onto what we have learned about God is how we stay on the right path.
verses.memory_phrase: I will not forget God's ways.
verses.prayer: God, help me hold onto what I learn about You and never forget it.
verses.discussion_question: Why is it important to remember what you learn about God now, while you are young?
verses.family_discussion: What are some of God's ways that you want your family to never forget?
verses.do_it_today: Write down or draw one important thing you have learned about God so you will not forget it.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child writing in a journal with a Bible nearby, golden light on the page, being careful to remember

verses.kjv: For her house inclineth unto death, and her paths unto the dead.
verses.little_bible: The path of going the wrong way leads to ruin — it is a road that does not end well.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Going the wrong way leads to very sad consequences.
verses.meaning: Wrong paths have real consequences. This is a serious warning that some choices lead toward destruction rather than life.
verses.memory_phrase: Wrong paths lead to sad endings.
verses.prayer: God, keep me away from paths that lead to harm.
verses.discussion_question: If a road had a sign that said 'This road leads to danger,' would you still walk on it?
verses.family_discussion: Why does God warn us about dangerous paths — is it because He is strict or because He loves us?
verses.do_it_today: Draw a road with a warning sign and write 'Choose God's path instead' at the bottom.
verses.illustration_prompt: A dark winding road with a faded warning sign, and a bright hopeful road beside it shining with light

verses.kjv: None that go unto her return again, neither take they hold of the paths of life.
verses.little_bible: When people keep going the wrong way, they find it very hard to turn back. That is why we must choose right from the beginning.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Wrong choices are hard to undo. Choose right from the start.
verses.meaning: Wrong choices can trap us more deeply over time. This underlines the urgency of choosing wisdom early — before bad habits take hold.
verses.memory_phrase: Choose right from the beginning.
verses.prayer: God, help me make right choices today before wrong ones become habits.
verses.discussion_question: Why is it easier to choose something right the first time than to change later?
verses.family_discussion: What habits is our family building now that will help us live well later?
verses.do_it_today: Choose one good habit to start today — even a very small one.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child at a crossroads choosing a bright path, with wise parents gently pointing the way

verses.kjv: That thou mayest walk in the way of good men, and keep the paths of the righteous.
verses.little_bible: God wants you to walk with good people and follow the paths of those who do what is right.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Walk with good people. Follow those who do right.
verses.meaning: The friends and role models we choose shape the path we walk. Following good people helps us stay on God's right path.
verses.memory_phrase: I walk with good and wise people.
verses.prayer: God, give me good friends who love You and help me walk the right way.
verses.discussion_question: Who are the good, wise people you want to be like?
verses.family_discussion: Who are the good examples in your family, church, or community?
verses.do_it_today: Spend time today with someone you admire for their good character.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child walking hand-in-hand with a kind adult along a sunny, open path

verses.kjv: For the upright shall dwell in the land, and the perfect shall remain in it.
verses.little_bible: People who do what is right will have a good place to live and belong. They will stay.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Those who do right will have a safe and good home.
verses.meaning: Living rightly brings lasting blessing. God honours those who are upright with security and a place of belonging.
verses.memory_phrase: Doing right brings a blessed home.
verses.prayer: God, help our family do what is right so that Your blessing stays with us.
verses.discussion_question: What kind of home do you want to live in when you grow up?
verses.family_discussion: How does choosing right, every day, build a blessed and stable home?
verses.do_it_today: Do one right, kind thing at home today to make it a better, warmer place.
verses.illustration_prompt: A cosy home glowing with warm light, surrounded by a peaceful garden, a happy family inside

verses.kjv: But the wicked shall be cut off from the earth, and the transgressors shall be rooted out of it.
verses.little_bible: But those who keep doing wrong will lose their place. Wrong choices have real and lasting consequences.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Wrong choices have serious consequences. God's way is the right way.
verses.meaning: This verse is a solemn warning that persistent wickedness leads to being cut off from blessing. God's justice is real, and He calls everyone to choose the right path.
verses.memory_phrase: God's way leads to life.
verses.prayer: God, let me always choose Your way so that I walk in blessing.
verses.discussion_question: What happens to a weed in a garden? How is that like choosing wrong over and over?
verses.family_discussion: How does God's warning here actually show His love for us?
verses.do_it_today: Thank God today that He has shown you the right path and that you can choose it.
verses.illustration_prompt: A garden with a healthy, glowing plant on the bright side and a withered weed on the dark side
